subject: Two clasped hands locked in a tight handshake or arm-grip, fingers interlaced and knuckles whitened with pressure, framed against a blood-red void
description: An extreme close-up on two human hands gripping one another in a fierce handshake — fingers laced, knuckles paled from pressure, veins and tendons standing out across the backs of the hands. A single bead of blood beads at one knuckle, the only sharp detail apart from skin texture. The arms emerge from darkness on either side and meet dead-center, framed against a saturated crimson backdrop that bleeds toward black at the edges. The crop excludes faces and bodies entirely, reducing the entire transaction to grip, sinew, and the implication of a wound. Lighting is low and warm, with a deep chiaroscuro that pushes everything but the clasped hands into shadow.
foreground: Two interlocked hands in a forceful handshake, a small drop of blood visible on a knuckle
background: Saturated blood-red void deepening to black at the edges
emotion: tension, pressure, mutual coercion — the grip is too tight to be friendly
